title: podman no longer respecting embedded CMD
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

Running an image with an embedded Cmd:

```
$ podman image inspect larsks/tinyproxy -f '{{.Config.Cmd}}'
[tinyproxy -d]
```

Results in the error:

```
$ podman run --rm larsks/tinyproxy
{"msg":"executable path not specified: Invalid argument","level":"error","time":"2020-04-20T04:05:01.000894733Z"}
```

Of course, there is a command specified (see the `inspect` output above) and the binary exists in the image:

```
$ podman run --rm larsks/tinyproxy tinyproxy -v
tinyproxy 1.10.0
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Build an image from the following Dockerfile:

  ```
  FROM alpine:latest
  CMD /bin/sh
  ```

2. Attempt to run the image:

```
$ podman run -it --rm testimage
{"msg":"executable path not specified: Invalid argument","level":"error","time":"2020-04-20T04:07:16.000699717Z"}
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            2.0.0-dev
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.13.9
Git Commit:         eb27ea70051afe415e9f4cb030991dda325306ad-dirty
Built:              Fri Aug  3 20:00:00 2018
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64

```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

  cpus: 8

  eventlogger: journald
  hostname: madhatter
  idmappings:

      size: 1
    - containerid: 1
      hostid: 100000
      size: 65536
  kernel: 5.5.17-200.fc31.x86_64
  memfree: 8627539968
  memtotal: 33574580224
  ociruntime:
    name: crun
    package: crun-0.13-2.fc31.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/crun
    version: |-
      crun version 0.13
      commit: e79e4de4ac16da0ce48777afb72c6241de870525
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
  os: linux
  rootless: true
  runtimeinfo: {}
  slirp4netns:
    executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns
    package: slirp4netns-1.0.0-1.fc31.x86_64
    version: |-
      slirp4netns version 1.0.0
      commit: a3be729152a33e692cd28b52f664defbf2e7810a
      libslirp: 4.1.0
  swapfree: 16890458112
  swaptotal: 16890458112
  uptime: 11m 39.45s
store:
  configfile: /home/lars/.config/containers/storage.conf
  containerstore:
    number: 2
    paused: 0
    running: 0
    stopped: 2
  graphdrivername: overlay
  graphoptions:
    overlay.mount_program:
      Executable: /usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs

        fusermount3 version: 3.6.2

        FUSE library version 3.6.2
        using FUSE kernel interface version 7.29

    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  imagestore:
    number: 100
  runroot: /run/user/1000/containers
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-2.0.0-1587204270.gite4e42b28.fc31.x86_64
```

comments.body: NB: This seems to be true for every podman-2.x.x version I've tried.

Podman 2.0 is very broken right now.  I have a PR open that I think might fix this.
https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/5862

> Podman 2.0 is very broken right now.

I figured that was probably the case. I'll stick with 1.9.x for now. I'll close this bug since I'm pretty sure the situation is already covered by your linked pr.
